hypothesis: Cache-Aware Hybrid Blending via Dynamic Tail Calls:** If we implement the SHOR-PSRO $\sigma_{hybrid}$ blending strategy by injecting `BPF_JMP_TAIL_CALL` instructions, we can dynamically switch between sorting kernels based on L1/L2 cache locality. This eBPF bytecode modification transitions execution from a scalar partitioning BPF program to a loop-unrolled, simulated SIMD block-move program (using fixed-size memory copies expanded to sequential `BPF_STX` and `BPF_LDX` instructions) when the active sorting partition fits within the L1 cache boundaries, maximizing memory throughput.
